feed.title: Xin
feed.subtitle: Interested in computer science, math and physics, and their connections.

entry.title: QCQI Solution Problem 4.3
entry.summary: Solution to problem 4.3 on page 212 Q1 Since $U$ is a unitary, all the eigenvalues have norm equal to $1$. \[U = \sum_{k=0}^{2^n-1}{e^{-i\theta_k} \ket{k} \bra k}, \quad \theta_k \in[0,2\pi)\] thus \[H \equiv i\ln{(U)}=\sum{ \theta_k \ket{k} \bra{k} }\] Q2 general idea First, define \[\mathbf{H}\equiv \{H\vert H:2^n\times 2^n \text{ Hermitian}\}\] \[\mathbf{P_n} \equiv \{I,X,Y,Z\}^{\o...

entry.title: QCQI Solution Chap4-7
entry.summary: Exercise 4.47 \[\begin{align} e^{i\sum{H_k}t}&=1+i(H_1+\cdots H_k)t-\frac{(H_1+\cdots H_k)^2}{2}t^2-i\frac{(H_1+\cdots H_k)^3}{6}t^3+\cdots\\ &=1+i(H_1+\cdots H_k)t-\frac{\sum{H_k^2}+\sum_{i,j;i\neq j}{(H_iH_j+H_jH_i)}}{2}t^2+\cdots\\ e^{iH_jt}&=1+iH_jt-\frac{H_j^2}{2}t^2-i\frac{H_j^3}{6}t^3+\cdots\\ e^{iH_kt}&=1+iH_kt-\frac{H_k^2}{2}t^2-i\frac{H_k^3}{6}t^3+\cdots \end{align}\]...

entry.title: QCQI Solution Chap4-5
entry.summary: Exercise 4.36 First two qubits represent x and last two qubits represent y Exercise 4.40 By definition: \[\begin{align} E(R_{\hat n}(\alpha),R_{\hat n}(\alpha+\beta)) & = \max_{\ket\psi}{\Vert{(R_{\hat n}(\alpha)-R_{\hat n}(\alpha+\beta))\ket\psi}\Vert} \\ & = \max_{\ket\psi}{\Vert{(I-R_{\hat n}(\beta))R_{\hat n}(\alpha)\ket\psi}\Vert} \\ & = \max_{\ket\phi}{\Vert{(I-R_{\ha...

entry.title: QCQI Solution Chap4-3
entry.summary: Exercise 4.20 Since \(H\otimes H={1\over \sqrt 2}\begin{bmatrix}H&H\\H&-H\end{bmatrix}\) Due to the fact that $H$ is hermitian and unitary, $HH=HH^\dagger=I$. Also using identity: $HXH=Z$, we have \[\begin{align} H\otimes H\cdot CNOT01 \cdot H\otimes H & = {1\over2}\begin{bmatrix}H&H\\H&-H\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix}I\\&X\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix}H&H\\H&-H\e...
